type.en: Reason why substance was added to the report

comments.comment: Reporting on CO associated with munition GHG/air releases is a new requirement for DND. Because of this, CFB Suffield now triggers the requirement for the report (as it exceeds the CO reporting threshold).

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: The level of military training activity for the year 2006 was significantly less than previous years.  Therefore, the amount of munitions fired down range was somewhat less, with the corresponding reduction in copper released during live firing exercises.

type.fr: Commentaire sur l'élimination

comments.comment: Lead is a component of many types of munitions fired at CFB Suffield by troopps on exercise.  The wide dispersal and destruction of rounds fired, renders it impossible to collect the remaining fragments for disposal.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: The level of military training activity was significantly less in 2006, resulting in less munitions being expended.  Hence, there was a sizable reduction in the release of lead from live firing exercises.

type.fr: Commentaire sur le recyclage

comments.comment: Lead is a component of many types of munitions fired at CFB Suffield by troops on exercise.  The wide dipersal and destruction of rounds fired, renders it impossible to collect these fragments for disposal or recycling.

comments.comment: The Base continues to collect approx 50% of the brass (composed of >70% copper) gernerated from live firing by British Army units.  It is being stored on site for eventual removal to the United Kingdom where it will be recycled.

type.fr: Commentaire sur l'élimination

type.fr: Commentaire sur le recyclage

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: The British and Canadian Armies train at CFB Suffield, although the British are the primary users.  Overall, there was considerably less military training activity in 2003 versus the year 2002, and in particular, Canadian activity was negligible in 2003.

type.fr: Commentaire sur l'élimination

comments.comment: Lead is a component of many types of munitions fired by troops in training on the military training area at CFB Suffield.  The wide dispersal and destruction of rounds fired, renders it impossible to collect these munitions for disposal or recycling.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: The training activity level at CFB Suffield in 2003 was less than year 2003evious year.  This was primarily the result of no large Canadian units conducting exercises as was the case one year earlier.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: The effect of energy management systems installed during 2002, have had the positive impact of reducing fuel consumption throughout the Base during the year 2003.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: The effect of energy management systems installed during 2002, have had the positive impact of reducing fuel consumption throughout the Base during the year 2003.

type.fr: Commentaire sur le recyclage

comments.comment: Approximately one half of the brass generated from live firing at the Base is recovered for recycling.  Roughly 8.1 tonnes of copper contained in brass ammunition shell casings was returned to the British Army in the United Kingdom for recycling last year

comments.comment: Lead is a component of many munitions fired by troops in training throughout the military training area.  The wide dispersal of fired rounds renders it impossible to collect these munitions for disposal or recycling.
